device_name: TISSUE OF ORIGIN TEST KIT FFPE
intended_use: The Pathwork® Tissue of Origin Test is an in vitro diagnostic intended to measure the degree of similarity between the RNA expression patterns in a patient's formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) t
intended_use_full: The Pathwork® Tissue of Origin Test is an in vitro diagnostic intended to measure the degree of similarity between the RNA expression patterns in a patient's formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tumor and the RNA expression patterns in a database of fifteen tumor types (poorly differentiated, undifferentiated and metastatic cases) that were diagnosed according to then current clinical and pathological practice. This test should be evaluated by a qualified physician in the context of the patient's clinical history and other diagnostic test results.
target_condition: tumors of unknown primary origin; poorly differentiated, undifferentiated, and metastatic tumors requiring classification
ground_truth: Prior test results obtained using the 510(k) cleared method (predicate device K092967) with ≥150 ng RNA; all concordant specimens agreed with available clinical diagnosis
other_metrics: Concordance: 97.7% (95% CI: 87.7%, 99.9%) for modified vs. unmodified procedure. Regression analysis slope: 0.91 (0.88, 0.93), R²: 0.94. Original predicate device (K092967) accuracy: 88.5% (85.3, 91.3). Interlab reproducibility (predicate): 88.5% overall concordance (83.1-93.2%) across 3 labs.
